Punjab PSTET Result 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download Scorecard

Quick Summary: State Council of Educational Research and Training (SCERT) Punjab on behalf of Punjab School Education Board (PSEB) has released Punjab PSTET Result 2026 on 7th April 2026 at official portal pstet2025.org. Candidates can download their scorecard for Paper 1 & Paper 2 using Registration Number & Password / Date of Birth. Check qualifying marks, merit list details, and next steps for eligibility certificate below.

Are you waiting for Punjab PSTET Result 2026? Great news! The Punjab State Teacher Eligibility Test (PSTET) 2026 result for Paper 1 (Classes I-V) and Paper 2 (Classes VI-VIII) has been officially declared today (7th April 2026). Lakhs of candidates who appeared in the examination held on 15th March 2026 across various centres in Punjab can now check their qualifying status and download scorecards online.

This article provides complete information about Punjab PSTET Result 2026 including direct download links, scorecard details, qualifying marks, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

Punjab PSTET 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Conducting Body SCERT Punjab / PSEB
Exam Name Punjab State Teacher Eligibility Test (PSTET) 2026
Result Type Paper 1 & Paper 2 Scorecard
Result Declaration Date 7th April 2026
Exam Conducted On 15th March 2026
Selection / Eligibility Qualifying Marks → Lifetime Valid TET Certificate
Official Website pstet2025.org

Check Your Result Now

Download PSTET Result 2026 Scorecard

Direct official link - Login with Registration Number & Password

How to Check Punjab PSTET Result 2026?

Candidates can download their PSTET 2026 scorecard by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: pstet2025.org
  2. Click on the “PSTET Result 2026” or “Candidate Login” link
  3. Enter your Registration Number and Password / Date of Birth
  4. Submit details to view your Paper 1 / Paper 2 marks and qualifying status
  5. Download and print the scorecard for future reference

Punjab PSTET Merit List 2026 - What’s Inside?

The PSTET merit list 2026 includes details of all qualified candidates. Separate qualifying lists are prepared for Paper 1 and Paper 2.

Scorecard / Merit List Contains:

  • Registration Number / Roll Number
  • Candidate Name
  • Father’s Name
  • Category (General / OBC / SC / ST / EWS)
  • Subject-wise Marks (Paper 1 or Paper 2)
  • Total Marks Obtained (Out of 150)
  • Qualifying Status (Qualified / Not Qualified)
  • Overall Rank (if applicable)

Qualifying Marks:

  • General Category: 90 marks (60%)
  • Reserved Categories: 82 marks (55%)

Qualified candidates will receive a lifetime-valid TET eligibility certificate for teacher recruitment in Punjab.

Punjab PSTET Scorecard 2026 - Information Breakdown

Your PSTET scorecard 2026 will display the following information:

Candidate Name As per application form
Registration Number Unique login ID
Exam Date 15th March 2026
Paper Type Paper 1 or Paper 2
Subject-wise Marks Child Development, Language, Mathematics, EVS/Social Science etc.
Total Marks Obtained Out of 150
Qualifying Status Qualified / Not Qualified

Important Instructions for Qualified Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save your scorecard immediately
  • ✓ Take multiple printouts for future reference
  • ✓ The TET certificate is valid for lifetime
  • ✓ Use the certificate for teacher recruitment applications in Punjab
  • ✓ Verify all details on scorecard - report discrepancies immediately
  • ✓ Keep registration details safe for future logins
  • ✓ Check official website regularly for any further updates

Documents Required on CG Vyapam Pharmacist Grade II Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):

    • Voter ID Card

  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies - same as uploaded in application)

Optional but Recommended:

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones and smartphones

  • Calculators and electronic devices

  • Bluetooth devices and earphones

Important Instructions for CG Vyapam Pharmacist Grade II Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card 2-3 days before exam date
  • Check exam center location on Google Maps
  • Plan your route and transportation
  • Arrange all required documents in advance
  • Keep backup copies of admit card
  • Read exam instructions carefully

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry closes 30 minutes before exam starts (No late entry allowed)
  • Follow dress code if specified in admit card
  • Carry water and light snacks for outside exam hall
  • Keep calm and focused

During Exam:

  • Follow all invigilator instructions carefully
  • Fill OMR sheet with black/blue ballpoint pen only
  • Manage your time wisely for all sections
  • Don't panic if questions seem difficult
  • Review answers if time permits
  • Don't carry prohibited items inside exam hall

What If CG Vyapam Pharmacist Grade II Admit Card Not Available?

If you're unable to download your CG Vyapam Pharmacist Grade II Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again with a fresh session
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Safari)
  3. Solution 3: Check your application status on the official website to verify if your application was accepted
  4. Solution 4: Contact CG Vyapam helpdesk immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues downloading admit card. Don't wait until exam day. Contact CG Vyapam helpdesk at least 2 days before the exam.

Selection Process

Selection will be made through Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by Scrutiny/Verification of Documents and Medical Examination.

  • Computer Based Test (CBT):
    • HEMM Operator (Trainee) (all posts): Common paper – 100 Marks, 90 minutes duration.
    • Paramedical Staff & Overseer (Civil): Separate paper for each post – 100 Marks (Section-A: 70 Marks Technical + Section-B: 30 Marks GK/Reasoning/Aptitude), 90 minutes duration.
    • No penalty for wrong answers. No marks for un-attempted questions.
    • Bilingual (English & Hindi). In case of any ambiguity in Hindi, English version will be final.
    • Minimum Qualifying Marks: UR/EWS – 50 marks; SC/ST/ESM/OBC(NCL)/PwBD – 40 marks.
    • Tie-breaking rules prescribed for each category of posts (matric marks, DOB, Section-A marks, alphabetical order etc.).
    • Indicative syllabus given in Annexure-A, B & C of the notification.
  • Document Scrutiny / Verification: Only candidates who qualify CBT as per cut-off will be called for verification of original documents/certificates in order of merit.
  • Medical Examination: Selected candidates must be found medically fit as per CIL Medical Attendance Rules.
  • Final selection is provisional and subject to verification of all documents, character & antecedents, and medical fitness.
  • Result and provisional merit panels will be published on NCL website.

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through the official website https://www.nclcil.in (Career > Recruitment > Apply Online). No other mode accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit https://www.nclcil.in → Career → Recruitment → Employment Notice/Advertisement for Direct Recruitment → Apply Online.
  2. Register and fill the online application form carefully with all mandatory details.
  3. Upload scanned copies of: Recent passport size photograph, signature, Matriculation certificate, HMV/Transport License (for HEMM posts), relevant Diploma/Degree/Registration certificates, Caste/EWS/PwBD/ESM certificates (if applicable).
  4. Pay the application fee online (if applicable).
  5. Submit the application and take a printout for future reference.
  6. Keep checking the NCL website regularly for updates, admit card, result etc.

General Instructions

  • Candidates must read the complete Employment Notice/Advertisement carefully before applying.
  • Cut-off date for determining eligibility (age, qualification, reservation etc.) is 01-05-2026.
  • Minimum/Essential qualifications acquired through distance learning/part-time shall not be considered.
  • Reservation & relaxation benefits will be given as per Govt. of India rules. Candidates must possess valid certificates in prescribed format.
  • All future correspondence/updates (admit card, result, notices etc.) will be available only on NCL website www.nclcil.in. Candidates are advised to visit the website regularly.
  • Mere issuance of admit card does not guarantee eligibility. Candidature remains provisional till document verification.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • Decision of NCL Management in all matters relating to eligibility, selection etc. shall be final and binding.
  • Court of jurisdiction for any dispute will be Hon’ble MP High Court, Jabalpur (M.P.).
  • Selected candidates can be posted in any mine/establishment/hospital of NCL and are liable for transfer to other subsidiaries of Coal India Limited.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for document verification or joining (except 2nd class Non-AC fare for SC/ST candidates for CBT as per rules).
